task_instructions: Use die-cut and electronic auxiliary material terminology when interpreting the TDS.
Treat thickness, adhesive thickness, color, adhesive type, and base material as high-frequency main fields when they are explicitly present.
Treat peel, shear, torque, pluck, liner options, shelf life, and method sections as parameter or method-card content unless routing says otherwise.
field_mapping_guidance: Main searchable fields should route to item core fields whenever the dictionary marks them as main fields.
Conditioned or chart-based performance metrics should go to spec_values, not to the main table.
Liner, storage, and shelf-life content may become parameter values or series notes depending on specificity.
brand_or_domain_conventions: Die-cut material documents often mix method explanation and values on the same page; split them into method cards plus parameter values.
Double-sided tapes and foam tapes often share series-level conclusions, but the draft should preserve that these are inferred as series-wide.
table_patterns: Thickness matrix rows are usually ordered by item code.
Performance tables may be grouped by substrate such as Painted Panel, PVC, or SUS.
negative_rules: Do not mistake chart legend colors for actual product color.
Do not treat Typical Results as guaranteed specification limits.
few_shot_examples: 180 Peel Adhesion to Painted Panel belongs to adhesion performance values, not a main field.
Liner Options SC2 or PET are liner configuration parameters or series notes.
